_comment: Chat-grounding eval: natural-language questions with expected doc-path substrings. Measures whether chat retrieval (blend + withText + K) surfaces the right page in the grounding sources. Run: npm run chat-eval. A case is a hit when any top source path contains any `expect` substring. Cases with a `history` array are multi-turn: the endpoint condenses history + q into a standalone question before retrieving (this exercises the condenser, and costs one small Haiku call per such case — single-turn cases remain LLM-free). Grow this from real ChatLog questions as traffic arrives.

cases.q: How do I define a table with a primary key?
How does replication work in Harper?
How do I store binary data like images?
How do I subscribe to real-time changes over MQTT?
How do I write a custom REST endpoint?
How do I cache data from an external source?
How do I deploy my application to Harper Fabric?
How do I add a vector index for similarity search?
How do I define a relationship between two tables?
How do I run TypeScript without a build step?
How do I query a table with the REST API using filters?
How do I load environment variables from a .env file?
How do I authenticate requests to Harper?
How do I configure mutual TLS (mTLS)?
How do I use the operations API?
How do I install and connect to Harper?
How do I add an index to a table attribute?
How do I serve a React or Vite front-end from Harper?
How do I run a Next.js app on Harper?
How do I configure Harper with a configuration file?
How do I monitor Harper with analytics?
How do I use write-through caching to an external database?
How do I set a TTL or expiration on records?
How do I define a component and its config.yaml?

cases._comment: ── Multi-turn cases: the follow-up `q` is meaningless on its own; the endpoint condenses history + q into a standalone question before retrieving. These give the condenser the same golden-set coverage as the single-turn cases above.
cases.q: what about for v4?

history.content: How does replication work in Harper?
Harper replication syncs data across nodes using a subscription model.

cases.q: how do I configure it?

history.content: How does replication work in Harper?
Harper replication syncs data across nodes.

cases.q: how do I query it?

history.content: How do I add a vector index for similarity search?
Add an @embed/vector attribute in your schema to build an HNSW index.

cases.q: can I add authentication to it?

history.content: How do I write a custom REST endpoint?
Define a Resource class and export it; Harper routes REST requests to it.

cases.q: how do I serve them back as images?

history.content: How do I store binary data like images?
Use the Blob data type to store binary payloads efficiently.

cases.q: what about write-through to an external database?

history.content: How do I cache data from an external source?
Harper supports integrated caching that pulls from an external source on miss.

cases.q: how do I create an account first?

history.content: How do I deploy my application to Harper Fabric?
Deploy your component to a Fabric cluster from the CLI or dashboard.

cases.q: does it need a build step?

history.content: Can I write my Harper components in TypeScript?
Yes — Harper runs TypeScript directly via Node's type stripping.

cases.q: which plugin loads them?

history.content: How do I use environment variables from a .env file?
Harper reads .env values into your app's configuration at startup.

cases.q: how do I query across them?

history.content: How do I define a relationship between two tables?
Declare a relationship attribute in your GraphQL schema linking the two tables.

cases.q: can I do the same over WebSockets?

history.content: How do I subscribe to real-time changes over MQTT?
Subscribe to a topic over MQTT and Harper pushes changes as they happen.

cases.q: how do I add an index to one of the columns?

history.content: How do I define a table with a primary key?
Declare the table in a GraphQL schema and mark the id field @primaryKey.

cases.q: and how do I set a TTL on the records?

history.content: How do I cache data from an external source?
Harper caches external data in a table and refreshes it on access.
